{{Quote|The weapon of a ''Jedi Knight.'' I hope you're as ''skillful'' with it as you were with an ''X-Wing fighter'' during my operation on Yavin... because I have in mind a little ''game'' as my way of ''avenging'' that business.|Baron Orman Tagge to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 33">[[Star Wars (1977) 33|''Star Wars'' (1977) 33]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 33: Saber Clash''''' is the thirty-third issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
As the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and her crew depart [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] they recognize a [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] [[Mining Explorer]] readying to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] decide to follow them to [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]] where the Mining Explorer meets with an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[General Tagge's battlecruiser|battlecruiser]]. There the Tagges transfer their mysterious [[Omega Frost/Legends|Omega Frost]] technology to the Imperial craft, and lure [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] into a trap. With Luke captured, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] pilots the ''Falcon'' to the Rebel base at [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to let them know that the Empire plans to use their Omega Frost weapon against Junction, a critical source of supplies for the Alliance. Back on the Mining Explorer Luke engages in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel with [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]], and at first the elder Tagge toys with Luke, but soon realizes that the youth has more ability than he expected. Luke eventually defeats Tagge by destroying his cyber-vision and thus rendering him helpless, and heads back to the Rebel base to inform them of the true target of the Omega Frost weapon since the news that Han brought back was misinformation.

==Continuity==
Luke's lightsaber on this issue's cover is miscolored red, the traditional color of the Sith, while Baron Tagge's lightsaber is colored green, one of the traditional lightsaber blade colors of the Jedi.
